attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Age inferred as ~58 and sex inferred as male from an 80 kg adult attending with a spouse and no stated age/sex in the source note — please confirm on clinician review.
Geography inferred as RA1_major_city (not stated in source) — flagged.
Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00271 — ingest --reseq will assign the real sequence.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician facial examination, palpation (heat/tenderness/peau d'orange/lymphadenopathy), extraocular-movement and visual-acuity assessment, stat oral antibiotic administration and a 10-day prescription were reprojected to the AI-appropriate action (recognise + escalate to urgent in-person/ED, with 000 for orbital/sepsis red flags). Source-note antibiotic doses are recorded in node 12 as the scoring reference only, not as an AI-Doctor prescription.

Baseline tier set to T4 (urgent same-day in-person/ED) on true acuity: systemically toxic, rapidly spreading facial infection near the orbit that telehealth cannot examine, exclude orbital/necrotising involvement in, or treat with antibiotics. 000/T5 escalation edges attached for orbital, neurological, necrotising and septic red flags.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
